core_rule: Ingestion must be idempotent: a document's content hash decides insert/update/skip, so re-running over unchanged docs is a no-op.

extracted_operations.name: attach-docstore
extracted_operations.trigger: Any ingestion path that runs more than once with no docstore wired.
extracted_operations.action: Pass docstore= (persisted Redis/Mongo/Postgres in prod, SimpleDocumentStore for tests) plus docstore_strategy=. The docstore is the dedup ledger keyed doc_id -> content_hash, separate from the vector store.
extracted_operations.output: Pipeline that can answer 'have I seen this content?' before embedding.

extracted_operations.name: pin-stable-doc-id
extracted_operations.trigger: Docs loaded with random/per-run ids; 're-run re-adds everything' symptom.
extracted_operations.action: Set filename_as_id=True or assign doc.id_ from a stable external identity (path/CMS id/URL). Never a fresh uuid per load.
extracted_operations.output: Same source doc hashes to same key across runs -> skip path can fire.

framework_mapping.manual: d.id from external identity, not uuid4()

extracted_operations.name: hash-before-embed
extracted_operations.trigger: Designing or reviewing the dedup decision point.
extracted_operations.action: Compute content hash and look it up in the docstore BEFORE the embedding transformation runs, so unchanged docs cost zero embedding tokens.
extracted_operations.output: Re-run cost on a steady corpus ~ 0 embedding calls.

framework_mapping.llamaindex: automatic given a docstore in the pipeline
framework_mapping.langchain: index() skips unchanged via RecordManager hash lookup

extracted_operations.name: pick-upsert-strategy
extracted_operations.trigger: Choosing pipeline behaviour for the corpus's change shape.
extracted_operations.action: UPSERTS (append-mostly), UPSERTS_AND_DELETE (mirror of shrinkable source; prod default), DUPLICATES_ONLY (in-run dedup only, NOT cross-run). Decide from change shape, not by default.

framework_mapping.manual: branch on prev hash; track seen set for delete

extracted_operations.name: propagate-deletes
extracted_operations.trigger: Source docs can be deleted and stale chunks must not be retrieved.
extracted_operations.action: Use UPSERTS_AND_DELETE; pipeline diffs doc_ids seen this run vs docstore and purges orphans. Gate on 'complete snapshot' to avoid purging on partial input.
extracted_operations.output: Deleted source docs -> vectors removed; retrieval stays in sync.

framework_mapping.manual: for orphan in stored_keys - seen: vs.delete + store.delete

extracted_operations.name: reingest-no-op-test
extracted_operations.action: Run the pipeline twice over identical input; assert the second run() returns 0 processed nodes and vector count unchanged.
extracted_operations.output: Regression gate proving idempotency is wired through.

framework_mapping.manual: assert no vs.add() calls on 2nd pass

extracted_operations.name: incremental-update-test
extracted_operations.action: Edit one doc -> assert exactly that doc's old chunks gone, new present, others untouched. Delete one (under UPSERTS_AND_DELETE) -> its chunks gone, neighbours intact.
extracted_operations.output: Proof that update/delete are surgical, not full-rebuild.

framework_mapping.llamaindex: hash-change -> update path; delete path of UPSERTS_AND_DELETE
framework_mapping.manual: delete-then-add on hash change; orphan purge

extracted_operations.name: persist-docstore
extracted_operations.trigger: SimpleDocumentStore used, or docstore not persisted after run.
extracted_operations.action: storage_context.persist(persist_dir=...) after each run, or use a server-backed docstore that auto-persists and is shared across workers.
extracted_operations.output: Cold start reads the ledger -> does not re-embed the world.

extracted_operations.name: manual-hash-upsert
extracted_operations.trigger: No framework available (raw vector-store SDK, custom loader).
extracted_operations.action: Maintain {doc_id: sha256(content)} next to the vector store; compute hash, compare, INSERT/UPDATE(delete-then-add)/SKIP; track seen ids for delete propagation; persist.
extracted_operations.output: Framework-free idempotent ingestion with the same contract.

dilemma_cases.title: Document changed slightly: re-embed all or chunk-diff?
dilemma_cases.resolution: Default to document-level hashing; if huge+frequently-edited, split source into smaller logical documents so hash granularity matches edit granularity. Chunk-level diffing only if profiling proves per-doc re-embed is the bottleneck.

dilemma_cases.title: Deleted source docs: purge or keep?
dilemma_cases.resolution: Purge with UPSERTS_AND_DELETE / cleanup='full', but behind a 'complete snapshot' guard + partial-input circuit breaker. Prefer soft delete for regulated corpora.

anti_patterns: from_documents() re-run every cron tick (no docstore -> duplicates)
volatile metadata (timestamps) in hashed payload -> constant re-embed
DUPLICATES_ONLY treated as a cross-run re-ingest strategy
upsert-only on a corpus whose files get deleted -> stale ghosts
blind UPSERTS_AND_DELETE on possibly-partial run input -> purges live content
no twice-run idempotency test in CI
swap embedding model and expect incremental upsert to fix old vectors
cross_framework_axes: where the hash ledger lives (docstore vs RecordManager vs manual table)
how 'did nothing' is observed (run()==[] vs num_skipped==N vs no add calls)
how deletes propagate (UPSERTS_AND_DELETE vs cleanup='full'/'incremental' vs seen-set diff)
durability of the ledger (persisted/server-backed vs ephemeral in-memory)
